embedding.qa_text: September 11, 1990 Peggy Bernardy; Attorney Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" st. Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Ms. Bernardy: This correspondence is a follow-up to our conversation on September 6, 1990. Upon behalf of two city council representatives, I am respectfully requesting written authorization to expend funds from their controlled committees for "office holder expenses." In particular, we are requesting approval to utilize the surplus funds to purchase the following items and services: 1. a computer; 2. vehicle window signs encouraging citizens to vote in the upcoming gubernatorial election; 3. video image training e.g. how to conduct yourself and effectively communicate their views during the City Council meetings. The city has just recently started televising City Council meetings on the local Cable tv access channel; 4. voter appreciation proclamations thanking residents for their participation in the election; 5. local cable television advertising in which the City Council members will speak about community issues; 6. the costs of producing news releases discussing community issues; 7. the costs associated with producing and distributing a newsletter e.g. postage and printing costs and; 8. the costs associated with surveying (telephone) the residents about community issues; The City Council representatives will not be advocating a position or candidacy. I would also like to request copies of any former correspondence issued by our organization to others regarding office holder expenses. Cordially, Mark Mark Neveau 2830 Barrington Terrace Fremont, California 94536 P.S. Thanks for taking the time to help me on the phone the other day. Believe me your information and patience came at a most opportune time!
